q: As Ingwanjawa got stronger and stronger, the villager feared him more and more.
a: The stronger Ingwanjawa got, the more the villagers feared him.

q: As she grows older, she becomes more serene.
a: The older she grows, the more serene she becomes.

q: If the hotel is expensive, the service is good.
a: The more expensive the hotel, the better the service.

q: If you get up early, you will feel fresh.
a: The earlier you get up, the fresher you will feel.

q: When a girl uses too much make-up, she looks more unnatural.
a: The more make-up a girl uses, the more unnatural she looks.

q: If you speak more English, you'll become more fluent.
a: The more English you speak, the more fluent you'll become.

q: When a person grows older, he becomes more forgetful.
a: The older a person grows, the more forgetful he becomes.

q: She grew old and she became talkative.
a: The older she grew, the more talkative she became.

q: If you want more money, you will have to do more work.
a: The more money you want, the more work you will have to do.

q: When you work fast, you make more mistakes.
a: The faster you work, the more mistakes you make.

q: They became more prosperous and their neighbours got more jealous.
a: The more prosperous they became, the more jealous their neighbours got.

q: As she grew old, she became more patient.
a: The older she grew, the more patient she became.

q: If the river is deeper, the danger is greater.
a: The deeper the river, the greater the danger.

q: If parents love their children more, they will suffer more.
a: The more parents love their children, the more they will suffer.

q: If you want more money, you will have to do more work.
a: The more money you want, the harder you will have to work.

q: He worked hard. He earned more money.
a: The harder he worked, the more money he earned.

q: If you work harder, you will succeed more.
a: The harder you work, the more you will succeed.

a: The scarcer the goods, the greater the demand for them.

q: If the position is high, they will get a big commission.

q: As the jokes become more humorous, the laughter becomes louder.
a: The more humorous the jokes become, the louder the laughter becomes.

q: If the sentence is short, I understand well.
a: The shorter the sentence, the better I understand.

q: If the wind is strong, the waves are big.
a: The stronger the wind is, the bigger the waves are.

q: When our headmaster is busy, he becomes impatient.
a: The busier our headmaster is, the more impatient he becomes.

q: If the position is high, the responsibility is heavy.
a: The higher the position is, the heavier the responsibility is.

q: When she is busy, she becomes more impatient.
a: The busier she is, the more impatient she becomes.

q: If you are younger, it is easier for you to learn new things.
a: The younger you are, the easier it is for you to learn new things.

q: The air gets thin when we climb high.
a: The higher we climb, the thinner the air gets.

q: If you work harder, you will earn more.
a: The harder you work, the more time we need to do it.

q: If the sum is difficult, we need more time to do it.

q: When my grandmother gets old, she becomes graceful.
a: The older my grandmother gets, the more graceful she becomes.

q: If you want much, you get little.
a: The more you want, the less you get.

q: As you get older, you get more aches and pains.
a: The older you get, the more aches and pains you get.

q: As Nyi Lay eats more, he gets fatter.
a: The more Nyi Lay eats, the fatter he gets.

q: If you smoke more, your health becomes worse.
a: The more you smoke, the worse your health becomes.

q: He took more medicine but he became weaker and weaker.
a: The more medicine he took, the weaker he became.

q: If as people becomes older, he has more experience.
a: The older a person becomes, the more experience he has.

q: When the man becomes lucky, he becomes rich.
a: The luckier the man becomes, the richer he becomes.

q: If the baby is hungry, it will cry loudly.
a: The hungrier the baby is, the more loudly it will cry.

q: As he wanted to finish the work quickly, he made more mistakes.
a: The more quickly he wanted to finish the work, the more mistakes he made.

a: The faster he can run, the less time he will need.

a: The louder you speak, the better it will be.

q: When the author is famous, his books are expensive.
a: The more famous the author (is), the more expensive his books (are).

q: If you drive more carefully, you will have few accidents.
a: The more carefully you drive, the fewer accidents you will have.

q: As the students are more and more disobedient, the teachers are more and more disappointed.
a: The more disobedient the students (are), the more disappointed the teachers (are).

q: When the night is dark, the danger is great.
a: The darker the night (is), the greater the danger (is).

q: The manager gets upset when his men become more and more dishonest.
a: The more dishonest his men become, the more upset the manager gets.

q: When he is angry, he speaks little.
a: The angrier he is, the less he speaks.
